name: DML-Bench
description: DML-Bench is a systematic benchmark for Derivative-enhanced Machine Learning (DML) covering four scientific-ML pillars: (i) a synthetic corpus of approximately 17,000 result cells across six function families (poly_trig, trig, step, heston, bachelier, barrier_bs), seven dimensions in [1, 50], six training-set sizes in [256, 32768], five gradient-noise levels, and five seeds; (ii) a 1,000-cell rMD17 molecular grid (10 molecules x 5 splits x 7 methods x 3 backbones: PaiNN, MLP, GATv2); (iii) PDEBench Burgers (1+1D) and Darcy (steady 2D) with two label regimes (bare and ic); (iv) a 1,440-cell SPY-options panel (240 temporal + 1,200 purged-CV, evaluated against both Black-Scholes and SVI supervisors). Each result cell records (function-family or dataset, dimension or grid, n_train, sigma-noise, seed, method) and reports test value MSE, test gradient MSE, training time, and run metadata. The benchmark answers when training neural networks with both function values and their derivatives helps versus hurts.

creator.name: [Anonymous]
creator.description: Author identity withheld during NeurIPS 2026 Evaluations and Datasets Track double-blind review; restored at camera-ready.

publisher.name: [Anonymous]
publisher.description: Publisher identity withheld during double-blind review.

rai:dataCollection: Synthetic samples are generated deterministically by seeded function generators in dml_benchmark/functions.py (Black-Scholes, Bachelier, Heston, polynomial-trigonometric, trigonometric, step, barrier_bs) and recorded by experiments/run_full_benchmark.py. The rMD17 molecular geometries and energies/forces are taken verbatim from the published rMD17 release (Christensen and von Lilienfeld 2020) under CC-BY-4.0 and processed by experiments/molecular/run_painn.py, experiments/molecular/run_mlp.py, and experiments/molecular/run_gatv2.py. PDEBench Burgers (1+1D) and Darcy (steady 2D) fields are taken verbatim from the PDEBench release (Takamoto et al. 2022) under Apache-2.0 and consumed by scripts/run_burgers.py and scripts/run_darcy.py. SPY end-of-day option chains (2020-2022) are sourced from a public Kaggle dump licensed CC0 and processed by experiments/real_data_spy/build_spy_dataset.py to produce the SPY temporal split and 5-fold purged walk-forward CV. No human-subject data is collected.
rai:dataCollectionType: Composite: deterministic synthetic generation; redistribution of published scientific datasets (rMD17, PDEBench); and processed redistribution of public CC0 financial market data (SPY).
rai:dataCollectionRawData: Synthetic data is regenerated on demand from seeds (no raw archive). rMD17 raw data: https://figshare.com/articles/dataset/rMD17/12672038 . PDEBench raw data: https://github.com/pdebench/PDEBench . SPY raw data: public Kaggle SPY end-of-day options dump (CC0); the processed feature panel ships in data/spy_options/spy_processed.npz.
rai:dataCollectionTimeframe: Software development: 2025-10 to 2026-05. Synthetic experiments: 2025-12 to 2026-05. SPY market window covered: 2020-01 to 2022-12. rMD17 source release: 2020. PDEBench source release: 2022.
rai:dataAnnotation: All labels are machine-generated. Synthetic cells carry analytical ground-truth values and gradients (closed-form; or COS-method / reflection-principle / high-k MC LRM at k = 100,000 for the discontinuous-payoff subset). rMD17 cells carry the published energies and per-atom forces. PDEBench cells carry the published function fields plus PDE-residual targets. SPY cells carry market-quoted option prices and Black-Scholes-implied or SVI-implied auxiliary Greeks (delta, gamma, vega, theta) computed by build_spy_dataset.py. No human annotators were used; no annotation guidelines, demographics, or compensation apply.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Not applicable: labels are produced by deterministic mathematical procedures (analytical formulas, numerical integration, or physical simulation). The SPY auxiliary Greeks are computed by inverting the Black-Scholes formula and a fitted SVI surface; both procedures are fully specified in experiments/real_data_spy/build_spy_dataset.py.
rai:dataAnnotationPlatform: None. No annotation platform or workforce was involved.
rai:dataAnnotationAnalysis: Not applicable. Label provenance is deterministic and reproducible from seeds and source code.
rai:dataAnnotationPerItem: Synthetic: per cell, ground-truth value and ground-truth gradient (vector of length d). rMD17: per cell, total energy and per-atom force vectors. PDEBench: per cell, function field on the simulation grid plus PDE-residual targets. SPY: per cell, market price and four Greeks (delta, gamma, vega, theta).
rai:dataUseCases: Intended for: (a) controlled study of when derivative supervision improves regression of smooth or piecewise-smooth scalar fields; (b) replication and extension of Sobolev training, Differential ML, and PINN training-recipe results; (c) sensitivity analysis on noise level, sample size, dimension, function family, and balancer hyper-parameters; (d) cross-pillar comparison (synthetic vs molecular vs PDE vs financial). Out of scope: production option pricing, real-time molecular dynamics deployment, or any safety-critical decision system.
rai:dataBiases: Synthetic data is unbiased by construction (deterministic generators with fixed seeds, full factorial across families x dims x sizes x noise x seeds). rMD17 inherits the original MD17 selection bias toward small flexible organic molecules. SPY-only finance results may overestimate gains for liquid US equity indices and underestimate them for less-liquid markets or non-equity instruments. PDEBench coverage of physics regimes is by design narrow (one diffusion coefficient for Burgers, one beta for Darcy).
rai:dataLimitations: Synthetic functions are smooth or piecewise-smooth in low dimensions; they do not capture realistic high-dimensional structure such as natural-image pixel correlations. The SPY options dataset is a single equity index over a finite calendar window (purged 5-fold CV with embargo) and may not transfer to other underlyings. The rMD17 subset covers ten small organic molecules; transfer to larger molecules or different chemistries is out of scope. PDEBench Burgers (1+1D) and Darcy (steady 2D) cover one viscosity and one beta value, respectively. Gradient labels for Heston and step functions are intentionally noisy or undefined and constitute deliberate failure cases.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. The benchmark contains no personal data, no protected attributes, no biometric or health information, and no human-subject content. All data is either deterministically synthetic, derived from published scientific datasets (rMD17, PDEBench), or aggregated public market data (SPY end-of-day option chains, CC0).
rai:dataSocialImpact: DML-Bench is a methodology benchmark on synthetic data and public scientific datasets. Direct social impact is small: no people, no protected attributes, no biometrics, no health data. Indirect impact: by clarifying when derivative supervision helps, the benchmark can reduce wasted compute and energy in training-recipe search across scientific machine-learning communities. Estimated full-grid energy: approximately 150 kWh on 2x NVIDIA A6000 GPUs (carbon estimate documented in paper Section 8). Misuse risk: the SPY price-prediction component is a research benchmark, not a production trading signal; deployment without rigorous out-of-sample evaluation could lead to financial loss for the deployer.
rai:dataLifeCycle: Code and synthetic generators are versioned in git (anonymous mirror at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/dml-bench-EB7F). Result JSONs are append-only; each cell is identified by (family/dataset, dim, n_train, noise, seed, method) and is reproducible from the runner script. The repository includes requirements-lock.txt and environment.yml for environment pinning. After camera-ready, the repository will be moved to a non-anonymous host (HuggingFace or Dataverse, decision pending) and a DOI will be minted. No deletion or expiry policy applies; superseded result files are archived under results/_archive_pre_publish_2026-05-03 with their original timestamps.
rai:responsibleUse: Users should: (a) cite the original sources of redistributed data (rMD17: Christensen and von Lilienfeld 2020; PDEBench: Takamoto et al. 2022; SPY: Kaggle CC0 dump); (b) not use SPY supervised-learning results as a trading signal without independent forward-test validation; (c) not use rMD17-derived models as a substitute for ab initio simulation in safety-critical chemistry applications; (d) report seeds, method, dimension, n_train, and noise level when citing individual cells, since headline numbers depend strongly on these axes; (e) preserve the noise-level and function-family axes when reporting headline DML gains, since the benchmark documents regimes where DML hurts (heston, step, sigma > sigma_star).
rai:syntheticData: The synthetic pillar (~17,000 result cells under results/tier3_benchmark and results/tier4_benchmark) is generated by deterministic, seeded function families. The rMD17, PDEBench, and SPY pillars are not synthetic.

distribution.name: DML-Bench Anonymous Repository
distribution.description: Full benchmark code, data generators, experiment runners, and result JSONs (zip archive of the full repository tree, with internal notes and .git removed).

distribution.name: DML benchmark library (dml_benchmark/)
distribution.description: Core Python library: function generators, training loops, loss balancers, metrics, I/O.

distribution.name: Per-pillar experiment runners (experiments/)
distribution.description: Pillar-specific runner scripts: synthetic grid, molecular (PaiNN/MLP/GATv2), and SPY temporal/purged-CV.

distribution.name: Orchestration scripts (scripts/)
distribution.description: Top-level orchestration and PDEBench (Burgers, Darcy) runners.

distribution.name: Paper LaTeX source (paper/)
distribution.description: LaTeX source for the accompanying paper (anonymous version).

distribution.name: Tier 3 synthetic benchmark results
distribution.description: Approximately 11,000 cells: 6 function families x 7 dimensions x 6 sample sizes x 5 noise levels x 5 seeds x methods (vanilla, dml_fixed, dml_fixed_half, dml_gradnorm, dml_relobralo, dml_warmup). One JSON per cell.

distribution.name: Tier 4 synthetic benchmark results
distribution.description: Approximately 1,100 cells extending Tier 3 with additional dimensions and sample sizes.

distribution.name: rMD17 PaiNN backbone results
distribution.description: rMD17 cells with PaiNN backbone: 10 molecules x 5 splits x 7 methods.

distribution.name: rMD17 MLP backbone results
distribution.description: rMD17 cells with MLP backbone: 10 molecules x 5 splits x 7 methods.

distribution.name: rMD17 GATv2 backbone results
distribution.description: rMD17 cells with GATv2 backbone: 10 molecules x 5 splits x 7 methods.

distribution.name: PDEBench Burgers results (bare regime)
distribution.description: Burgers 1+1D cells trained without IC supervision (bare).

distribution.name: PDEBench Burgers results (ic regime)
distribution.description: Burgers 1+1D cells trained with initial-condition supervision (ic).

distribution.name: PDEBench Darcy results (bare regime)
distribution.description: Darcy 2D steady-state cells trained without IC supervision (bare).

distribution.name: PDEBench Darcy results (ic regime)
distribution.description: Darcy 2D steady-state cells trained with IC supervision (ic).

distribution.name: SPY options results (temporal split, Black-Scholes supervisor)
distribution.description: SPY options cells with temporal train/test split (train <= 2021-06, test >= 2021-07, 5-day embargo) and BS-derived Greeks as supervisor. Default n_train = 10,000 with 5 seeds and 5 methods.

distribution.name: SPY options results (temporal split, SVI supervisor option A)
distribution.description: Temporal-split SPY cells using SVI-implied Greeks (option A).

distribution.name: SPY options results (temporal split, SVI supervisor option C)
distribution.description: Temporal-split SPY cells using SVI-implied Greeks (option C).

distribution.name: SPY options results (purged walk-forward CV, Black-Scholes supervisor)
distribution.description: Purged 5-fold walk-forward SPY cells with embargo (BS supervisor). Cell name pattern spy_cv_n{N}_s{seed}_f{fold}_{method}.json.

distribution.name: SPY options results (purged walk-forward CV, SVI supervisor option A)
distribution.description: Purged 5-fold walk-forward SPY cells with SVI-implied supervisor (option A).

distribution.name: SPY options results (purged walk-forward CV, SVI supervisor option C)
distribution.description: Purged 5-fold walk-forward SPY cells with SVI-implied supervisor (option C).

distribution.name: SPY options processed feature panel
distribution.description: Preprocessed SPY end-of-day options panel (2020-2022): contracts with features [moneyness, T, r, iv, log_volume], Black-Scholes prices, and trading dates.

distribution.name: High-fidelity gradient references
distribution.description: Analytical and high-k MC gradient references for the synthetic discontinuous-payoff subset (barrier_bs reflection principle, heston_digital COS method, basket_d7 LRM at k=100,000).

recordSet.name: Synthetic benchmark results
recordSet.description: One record per synthetic experiment cell: function family x dimension x n_train x noise x seed x method.

field.name: method
field.description: Training method (vanilla, dml_fixed, dml_fixed_half, dml_gradnorm, dml_relobralo, dml_warmup, etc.).

field.name: func_type
field.description: Function family (poly_trig, trig, step, heston, bachelier, barrier_bs).

field.name: seed
field.description: Random seed (one of 5 fixed integers per cell).

field.name: test_value_mse
field.description: Test MSE of predicted function values.

field.name: test_grad_mse
field.description: Test MSE of predicted gradients (autograd) against high-fidelity references.

field.name: best_epoch
field.description: Epoch at which best validation loss was achieved.

field.name: time_s
field.description: Total training wall-clock time in seconds.

field.name: n_epochs_actual
field.description: Number of training epochs actually executed (with early stopping).

recordSet.name: rMD17 molecular benchmark results
recordSet.description: One record per molecular cell: backbone x molecule x split x method. Backbone is implicit per FileSet (PaiNN, MLP, GATv2).

field.name: molecule
field.description: Molecule name (one of 10 rMD17 molecules: aspirin, azobenzene, benzene, ethanol, malonaldehyde, naphthalene, paracetamol, salicylic_acid, toluene, uracil).

field.name: test_value_mse
field.description: Test MSE of predicted total energies.

field.name: test_grad_mse
field.description: Test MSE of predicted per-atom forces.

recordSet.name: PDEBench (Burgers and Darcy) benchmark results
recordSet.description: One record per PDE cell: equation x label-regime x method x seed.

field.name: test_value_mse
field.description: Test MSE of predicted PDE solution field.

recordSet.name: SPY options benchmark results
recordSet.description: One record per SPY options cell: split-mode x supervisor (BS or SVI) x method x n_train x seed x (fold for purged CV).

field.name: fold
field.description: CV fold index (0..4 for purged_walkforward; absent for temporal). The same field schema applies to spy-purged-bs-results, spy-purged-svi-A-results, spy-purged-svi-C-results, spy-temporal-svi-A-results, and spy-temporal-svi-C-results FileSets.

field.name: test_value_mse
field.description: Test MSE for option price prediction.

field.name: test_grad_mse
field.description: Test MSE for predicted Greeks (delta, gamma, vega, theta) against the BS or SVI supervisor.

recordSet.name: Benchmark methods enumeration
recordSet.description: Closed enumeration of training methods evaluated across pillars.

data.benchmark-methods/description: Standard MSE training without derivative supervision.
DML with fixed lambda=1 weighting (Huge and Savine 2020).
DML with GradNorm adaptive loss balancing (Chen et al. 2018).
DML with ReLoBRaLo balancing (Bischof and Kraus 2021).
Vanilla pre-training phase followed by DML fine-tuning.
Softmax-temperature balancer between value and derivative losses.

recordSet.name: Synthetic function families enumeration
recordSet.description: Closed enumeration of the six synthetic-pillar function families.

data.function-families/description: Sum of high-frequency trigonometric components per dimension.
Piecewise-constant step function (deliberate failure case for DML).
Heston stochastic-volatility option pricer with noisy MC Greeks (deliberate failure case).
Bachelier (normal model) option pricer with analytical Greeks.
Black-Scholes barrier option with reflection-principle Greeks.
